description_long: This object is a dark, stone-like pagoda structure with a square base and a series of stacked, receding tiers that form a pyramidal silhouette. Each tier is defined by pronounced horizontal ledges, and the structure is topped with a tall, pointed spire that features a bulbous ornament at its base. The central section of the pagoda has a prominent arched opening, suggesting a symbolic or ceremonial passageway. The surface appears weathered and textured, with a matte, rough finish typical of carved stone or concrete. The overall form is symmetrical, with each side mirroring the others, and the design is reminiscent of traditional Southeast Asian or Nepalese stupa architecture. The object is solid and monumental, likely intended as a decorative or spiritual landmark in an urban park or plaza.
description: A dark, stone pagoda with stacked tiers, a central arched opening, and a tall pointed spire, featuring a rough, weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central arched opening and the full height of the spire, with clear visibility of the tiered structure.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more depth to the base and the layered ledges, with the spire still prominent.
description_view_2: Rear or alternate side view, similar to the front but with less emphasis on the opening, highlighting the symmetry of the tiers.
description_view_3: Another side view, nearly identical to view 2, emphasizing the uniformity and solid construction of the pagoda.
